channel.title: Labour Law on Ventryx – Visitor & Attendance Management Software India
channel.description: Recent content in Labour Law on Ventryx – Visitor & Attendance Management Software India

item.title: Shift Management in India: How to Handle Night Shifts, Overtime, and Comp-Off Without Spreadsheets
item.description: Walk into any large manufacturing plant in Pune, a BPO operation in Hyderabad, a private hospital in Chennai, or a retail chain in Delhi NCR, and you will find one thing in common: attendance management is exponentially harder than it looks on paper. When your workforce runs across two or three shifts around the clock, the complexity of tracking who worked when, who earned overtime, and who is owed a comp-off grows faster than most spreadsheets can handle.

item.title: Leave Types in India Explained: CL, EL, SL, Comp-Off and How to Manage Them All
item.description:
If you have ever tried to explain your company’s leave policy to a new employee and watched their eyes glaze over halfway through, you are not alone. Leave management in India is genuinely complex - not because companies make it so deliberately, but because the underlying legal framework layers central legislation on top of state-specific rules, and most organisations add their own policies on top of that.

The result is that even experienced HR professionals sometimes get the rules wrong. Casual leave and earned leave get conflated. Encashment is offered when it should not be, or denied when it should be mandatory. Carryover limits are set informally and then disputed when an employee tries to claim them.

item.title: Overtime Management in India: What Every HR Manager Needs to Know
item.description: Overtime is one of the most mismanaged aspects of Indian payroll - not because HR managers are careless, but because the rules are genuinely complicated. The Factories Act says one thing. The Shops and Establishments Act in your state may say something different. What applies to a manufacturing worker does not apply to an IT professional. What is mandatory in Tamil Nadu may be discretionary in Delhi. And on top of all this, company policy sometimes diverges from statutory minimums in ways that create their own compliance obligations.
